# Session Keys

<Aside type="caution">
**Planned Feature** -- Session keys are not yet implemented in the Varity SDK. This page describes the planned functionality and current status.
</Aside>

## What Are Session Keys?

Session keys are temporary, scoped credentials that allow your app to perform operations on behalf of users without requiring approval for each action. They are a feature of smart account (account abstraction) systems.

When available, session keys will enable:

- **Batch operations** -- Execute multiple actions in one step
- **Background operations** -- Perform tasks without repeated user prompts
- **Scoped permissions** -- Limit what actions a session key can perform
- **Time-limited access** -- Keys expire after a set duration

## Current Status

Session keys require smart account (account abstraction) infrastructure. Here is the status of the underlying components:

| Component | Status | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| `SmartWalletProvider` | Source exists, not yet exported | 630+ line implementation using thirdweb v5, present in UI Kit source |
| `useSmartWallet` | Source exists, not yet exported | Hook for smart wallet operations, present in UI Kit source |
| Session key API | Not yet implemented | Will be added on top of smart account infrastructure |
| Privy embedded wallets | Available now | Current account system, does not support session keys |

The `SmartWalletProvider` and `useSmartWallet` are implemented in the `@varity-labs/ui-kit` source code but are currently commented out from the public exports. Session key functionality will be built on top of this smart account layer once it is publicly released.

## What You Can Do Today

While session keys are not yet available, you can use Privy's embedded wallet system for user authentication and transactions:

```tsx title="src/components/UserActions.tsx"
import { usePrivy, useWallets } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

function UserActions() {
  const { authenticated } = usePrivy();
  const { wallets } = useWallets();

  if (!authenticated) {
    return <p>Please sign in first</p>;
  }

  return (
    <div>
      <p>You are signed in with an embedded wallet.</p>
      <p>Each transaction requires user approval.</p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

For sending transactions, use the `useSendTransaction` hook from Privy:

```tsx
import { useSendTransaction } from '@privy-io/react-auth';

function ActionButton() {
  const { sendTransaction } = useSendTransaction();

  const handleAction = async () => {
    const result = await sendTransaction({
      to: '0x...',
      data: '0x...',
    });
    console.log('Transaction hash:', result.hash);
  };

  return <button onClick={handleAction}>Execute Action</button>;
}
```

## Planned Session Key API

The following is a preview of the planned session key API. This API is subject to change and is **not yet available**.

```tsx
// PLANNED -- NOT YET AVAILABLE
// This shows the intended API shape for session keys

import { useSmartWallet } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

function SessionExample() {
  const { createSessionKey, revokeSessionKey } = useSmartWallet();

  const handleCreate = async () => {
    const session = await createSessionKey({
      permissions: ['transfer', 'approve'],
      duration: 3600, // seconds
      maxTransactions: 100,
    });
    console.log('Session key created:', session.address);
  };

  return <button onClick={handleCreate}>Create Session Key</button>;
}
```

<Aside type="note">
The planned API shown above is for illustration only. The actual API may differ when session keys are released.
</Aside>
